what_it_does: Records a design stance on a gesture interaction: endorsing the core concept of a continuous hold-to-modulate-scale/batch-then-submit gesture while rejecting two specific implementation choices, and prescribing an alternative tap/long-press, stepped detents, live outline, best-effort haptics, arc-shaped thumb-zone V, with explicit risk treatment for iOS Safari gesture conflicts and web haptics limitations.
observations: Frontmatter identifies the item as a current block filed by ch-3mpkjg3r in channel dragnet-development, part_of board://item-a5b7cf79.
Title 'P9 · One-line stance' and body are concise, opinionated design feedback.
It distinguishes an underlying good idea from wrong specifics.
It names concrete interaction mechanics: tap-for-default, long-press-to-escalate, stepped detents, live outline, best-effort haptics, arc-not-circle V in configurable thumb zone.
It flags iOS-Safari gesture conflicts and no-web-haptics as device-verified risks, not assumed-green.
standouts: The 'keep the idea, retune the specifics' framing.
Arc-not-circle V in configurable thumb zone.
Explicit call to treat iOS-Safari gesture conflicts and no-web-haptics as first-class risks.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, history.
Markdown body with a heading and a single dense paragraph.
Links use kind 'part_of' to a parent board item.
concerns: The stance is prescriptive but provides no data or references to support the claim that vertical free-drag or long-press-as-primary are wrong.
No device-verified evidence is included in the file itself, only an assertion that risks should be device-verified.
The 'one-line stance' is actually a multi-clause paragraph, which may obscure scannability.
notes: A terse design verdict that separates the valuable gesture concept from its problematic implementation details and demands first-class risk handling for iOS Safari and web haptics.
questions: What is the parent item-a5b7cf79 and how does this stance relate to it?
What is the 'arc-not-circle V' geometry relative to the thumb zone?
Which specific iOS-Safari gesture conflicts are anticipated?
What haptics fallback is intended under 'best-effort'?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title 'One-line stance' and content match: it delivers a single, compact design position; the file does that, though the sentence is long.
intention_signals: To record a decisive, file-local design opinion that preserves a teammate's conceptual contribution while redirecting concrete mechanics and forcing unresolved platform risks to be verified rather than assumed.
novelty: It frames interaction feedback as 'right idea, wrong specifics' and explicitly elevates iOS-Safari gesture conflict and absent web haptics from implementation details to first-class, device-verified risks.
contribution: A compact, reusable design stance that a fused system can keep as a decision record: it captures the rationale for rejecting vertical free-drag/long-press-primary, the preferred tap/long-press/detent/haptic/arc-V mechanics, and the requirement to verify iOS Safari and web-haptics risks before treating them as solved.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item records a one-line design stance endorsing a continuous hold-to-modulate-scale gesture concept while rejecting vertical free-drag and long-press-as-primary, proposing tap-for-default/long-press-to-escalate with stepped detents, live outline, best-effort haptics, and an arc-shaped thumb-zone V, and treating iOS-Safari gesture conflicts and no-web-haptics as first-class device-verified risks.
intention_for_embedding: To capture a concise, file-local interaction-design verdict and risk framing for a gesture feature under development.
